  → The Roca brothers

  → El Bulli alumni creating the most exciting tasting menu in Spain. Possibly the world.
Disfrutar | Eixample | Creative Spanish | $$$$ | ★9.8 | Michelinstar, tastingmenu, reservationrequired, bucketlist
  → Three former elBulli chefs, three Michelin stars, and a tasting menu of hallucinatory creativity — macaroni made with ham jelly, ember-roasted sweet potato with its own foam. Bucket list dining.
Cocina Hermanos Torres | Les Corts | Creative Spanish | $$$$ | ★9.5 | Michelinstar, tastingmenu, reservationrequired, bucketlist
  → Twin chefs Sergio and Javier Torres in a breathtaking open-kitchen warehouse space — two Michelin stars for a tasting menu where watching them work is as spectacular as the food itself.

  → The family that invented the bomba de patata still fries them in Barceloneta. Cash only.

  → Newly Michelin-starred in 2025 — a Japanese-Mediterranean tasting menu that Time Out had been championing since day one. Barcelona
La Cova Fumada | Barceloneta | Seafood | $ | ★9.3 | walk-ins, cashonly, lunchonly, locallegend
  → The Barceloneta bar credited with inventing the bombas (meat-filled potato croquette) — no sign on the door, cash only, lunch service until the food runs out. One of the great unsung Barcelona institutions.
Quimet & Quimet | Poble Sec | Tapas | $ | ★9.2 | sharingplates, locallegend, beenhereforever, walk-insonly
  → Standing-room-only bodega where the fourth-generation Quimet serves conservas to packed crowds.

Cal Pep | El Born | Seafood Tapas | $$$ | ★9.2 | tapas, counterseating, walk-ins, seafood
  → One of the great Barcelona experiences — a single counter, no reservations, daily changing seafood from the market, and chef Pep Manubens behind the pass. The gambas al ajillo alone justify the trip.

  → The other 2025 Michelin star newcomer Time Out had long championed — a theatrical kitchen & bar concept where creative small plates and an extraordinary cocktail program make for one of Barcelona

  → A sandwich spot from the Bar Mut team staffed by bow-tied veterans who remember when pocket calculators were new — the calamari baguette is the best in Barcelona, full stop.

### Dallas (131 spots)
Otaka | Preston Hollow | Japanese | $$$$ | ★9.5 | omakase, counterseating, reservationrequired, datenight
  → Eight seats in a 1950s strip mall. Chef Masayuki Otaka
Lucia | Bishop Arts | Italian | $$$ | ★9.4 | pasta, datenight, naturalwine, reservationrequired
  → The best restaurant in Dallas, full stop. Seasonal housemade pasta and house-cured salumi in a romantic Bishop Arts space — no two visits are ever the same.
Cattleack Barbecue | Far North Dallas | BBQ | $ | ★9.3 | BBQ, walk-insonly, bucketlist, brisket
  → Open only Thursday and Friday, Cattleack is among the best barbecue operations in Texas — the brisket and jalapeño cheese sausage are legendary.
Mister Charles | Uptown | American | $$$$ | ★9.2 | scene, datenight, specialoccasion, reservationrequired
  → Peak Dallas in the best way — few restaurants are this expensive, and even fewer feel this good. The happiness is palpable.

  → A Richardson Neapolitan pizzeria that appeared from nowhere and immediately became one of the best pies in North Texas.

  → A tiny East Dallas Thai noodle shop from a Chiang Rai family that

  → A beloved East Dallas Vietnamese restaurant where the cooking is creative, the wine is natural, and the neighborhood regulars are fiercely loyal.
